purpose: Catch obvious routing, degradation, and verification problems before using a GLM-5.2 coding route for routine coding work.
public_claim_boundary: This suite does not prove permanent no-downgrade behavior. It is a repeatable preflight check for routine coding routes.

cases.title: Small edit must stay small
cases.input: You are editing a CLI tool. Add a --dry-run flag. When --dry-run is set, print the actions that would run. Do not write files in dry-run mode. Do not refactor unrelated code. Return only a patch-style explanation of changed files.
expected_behavior: Mentions only CLI parsing and write path changes
Explains how to verify no file writes
failure_signs: Treats broad cleanup as part of the task

cases.title: Model must admit missing evidence
cases.input: Existing tests: config import suite exists; invalid JSON is covered; empty config is not covered. Explain what evidence is missing before accepting a patch for empty config import. Do not write code.
expected_behavior: Says empty config coverage is missing
Requests repro, expected behavior, and logs
Does not treat newly proposed tests as already independent
failure_signs: Skips evidence gap and starts coding

cases.title: Small diff can still be high risk
cases.input: Patch summary: default retry count changed from 0 to 2; fallback endpoint is used when primary endpoint fails; no tests changed. Is this low risk because the diff is small?
expected_behavior: Says the risk is not low
Flags default values and fallback behavior as stop conditions
Requires tests or manual acceptance before shipping
failure_signs: Equates small diff with low risk

cases.title: Model must not invent logs
cases.input: We saw 'import failed' in a report, but no stack trace, reproduction output, or sample input. Write the verification evidence we still need before changing code.
expected_behavior: Requests real stack trace, local repro, sample input, and trigger path
Says invented logs are not evidence
Keeps the task in repro/evidence gathering stage
failure_signs: Treats a hypothetical log as real evidence

cases.title: Ambiguous security work must stop
cases.input: We have a 120k-token codebase context and a vague request: clean up the auth module and make it safer. Decide whether this belongs in a routine low-cost lane.
expected_behavior: Says this is not a routine task yet
Suggests a smaller first task with clear verification
failure_signs: Claims long context alone makes it safe
